capabilities.title: Register a session
capabilities.how_we_test: We run the add command to register a new session, then read the saved registry back. We confirm the row exists with the title, tool, group, and folder we asked for.
capabilities.assertion: new registry row carries the given title, tool, group, and working directory
capabilities.snapshot:
Profile: default

TITLE                GROUP           PATH                                     ID
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cap-add              capgrp          /tmp/TestCapability_Lifecycle_Add3841... 18761039-177

Total: 1 sessions

capabilities.title: Start a session
capabilities.how_we_test: We register a session and start it. We then ask the throwaway tmux server whether a live pane actually appeared, and confirm the registry flips the session to an active state.
capabilities.assertion: a real tmux pane appears on the isolated socket and status becomes active
capabilities.snapshot:
IDLE (1):
  ○ cap-start        shell      -                      ~/project

Total: 1 sessions in profile 'default'

capabilities.title: Stop a session
capabilities.how_we_test: We start a session, then stop it. We confirm the tmux pane is gone and the registry returns the session to the stopped state.
capabilities.assertion: tmux pane disappears and registry status returns to stopped
capabilities.snapshot:
Profile: default

TITLE                GROUP           PATH                                     ID
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cap-stop             001             /tmp/TestCapability_Lifecycle_Stop156... 5f61729b-177

Total: 1 sessions

capabilities.title: Restart a session
capabilities.how_we_test: We start a session and restart it. We confirm exactly one pane exists afterward (no accidental duplicate) and the session is active again.
capabilities.assertion: exactly one pane remains after restart and status is active (guards the #30 double-spawn)
capabilities.snapshot:
IDLE (1):
  ○ cap-restart      shell      -                      ~/project

Total: 1 sessions in profile 'default'

capabilities.title: Remove a session
capabilities.how_we_test: We confirm a stopped session can be removed and disappears from the registry, and that removing a still-running session is refused unless forced, so it is not destroyed by accident.
capabilities.assertion: stopped session leaves the registry; a running session is refused without force
capabilities.snapshot:
$ agent-deck session remove cap-rm-running
Error: session 'cap-rm-running' is in state 'starting'; only stopped/error sessions may be removed without --force

$ agent-deck list   (after stopping and removing cap-rm-stopped)
Profile: default

TITLE                GROUP           PATH                                     ID
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cap-rm-running       001             /tmp/TestCapability_Lifecycle_Rm21683... c04ab467-177

Total: 1 sessions

capabilities.title: Launch in one step
capabilities.how_we_test: We use the single launch command, which creates, starts, and messages a session at once, pointed at the stand-in echo agent. We confirm the registry row exists and the echoed message shows up on screen.
capabilities.assertion: one launch command creates the row, starts the pane, and the echoed message appears

capabilities.title: Fork guard
capabilities.how_we_test: Forking is only valid for supported tools with live context (Claude session ID, OpenCode session ID, Pi JSONL, or Codex rollout). We confirm forking an unsupported session is cleanly refused and creates no orphan child row. The full context-inheriting fork is a documented nightly gap.
capabilities.assertion: forking an unsupported session is refused and no child row is created
capabilities.snapshot:
$ agent-deck session fork cap-fork
Error: session 'cap-fork' is not a forkable session (tool: shell)

capabilities.title: Send a message to an agent and read its reply
capabilities.how_we_test: We launch a tiny stand-in agent that simply repeats whatever you say. We send it a unique message through the normal send command, then read the screen back. If the screen shows the echoed message we know it reached the agent and a reply came out the other side.
capabilities.assertion: the pane shows ECHO:<token> after a real send, proving readiness, send-keys, and capture read-back

capabilities.title: Real agent round trip (Claude)
capabilities.how_we_test: We launch a real Claude session, wait for its prompt, send a fixed instruction, and check the reply. This needs a real API key and network, so it runs nightly, not on every release.
capabilities.assertion: a real Claude reply contains the expected token

capabilities.title: Fork inherits tool context
capabilities.how_we_test: We fork live Claude, OpenCode, Pi, and Codex sessions and confirm each child inherits the conversation with a distinct id and a parent link. This needs real tool session data from live transcripts, so it runs nightly.
capabilities.assertion: child session links the parent and inherits conversation context

capabilities.title: Attach and detach an MCP
capabilities.how_we_test: We register a stub tool server, attach it to a session, and read the session's .mcp.json file back to confirm the entry was written. We then detach it and confirm the entry is gone. We also confirm attaching an unknown server is refused and never writes a broken config.
capabilities.assertion: .mcp.json gains the server entry on attach and loses it on detach; an unknown server is refused

capabilities.title: MCP actually loads in the agent
capabilities.how_we_test: We attach a tool server and ask a real agent to list its tools, confirming the agent honors the attachment. This needs a real agent to introspect, so it runs nightly.
capabilities.assertion: the agent lists the attached MCP server

capabilities.title: Create and finish a git worktree
capabilities.how_we_test: Against a throwaway git repo, we create a session on a new branch in its own worktree and confirm the worktree directory really exists on disk. We then run finish, and confirm the worktree and branch are removed, the session is gone, and the ORIGINAL repository is untouched.
capabilities.assertion: the worktree dir is created on its own branch, then finish removes it and the session while leaving the source repo intact (the #1200 data-loss guard)
capabilities.snapshot:
$ agent-deck worktree info cap-wt   (after add --worktree -b)
Session:        cap-wt
Branch:         feature/capfeature
Worktree Path:  ~/repo/.worktrees/feature-capfeature
Main Repo:      ~/repo
Status:         exists

$ agent-deck list   (after worktree finish: session gone, repo intact)
No sessions found in profile 'default'.

capabilities.title: Organize sessions into groups
capabilities.how_we_test: We create sessions in two different groups and confirm that filtering the registry by group returns exactly that group's members, with no session bleeding across groups.
capabilities.assertion: each group lists exactly its own sessions; no cross-group leakage
capabilities.snapshot:
Groups:

NAME                 SESSIONS   STATUS
--------------------------------------------------
alpha                2          ○ 2
beta                 1          ○ 1

Total: 2 groups, 3 sessions

capabilities.title: Keep profiles isolated
capabilities.how_we_test: We add one session under the default profile and another under a separate profile, then list each profile. We confirm neither profile can see the other's session.
capabilities.assertion: a session in one profile is invisible to the other (no cross-profile data bleed)
capabilities.snapshot:
$ agent-deck list   (default profile)
Profile: default

TITLE                GROUP           PATH                                     ID
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
in-default           001             /tmp/TestCapability_Profiles_Isolatio... 7eca0dea-177

Total: 1 sessions

$ agent-deck -p capalt list   (isolated profile)
Profile: capalt

TITLE                GROUP           PATH                                     ID
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
in-capalt            001             /tmp/TestCapability_Profiles_Isolatio... 625039ff-177

Total: 1 sessions

capabilities.title: Bring multiple tool kinds to readiness
capabilities.how_we_test: We launch two distinct stand-in tools and confirm each one reaches an active state AND echoes back the unique message we sent it, proving the launch and readiness machinery is not tied to a single tool.
capabilities.assertion: two different tools each reach active and echo their token back
capabilities.snapshot:
tool "echobot" (cap-tool-a):
ECHOBOT READY > PINGA-cap-e2e
ECHO:PINGA-cap-e2e

tool "parrot" (cap-tool-b):
ECHOBOT READY > PINGB-cap-e2e
ECHO:PINGB-cap-e2e

capabilities.title: Worker reports it finished
capabilities.how_we_test: A worker prints a completion sentinel on its last turn. We run the real Stop-hook handler and confirm it records a done outcome (status and summary), then run the notifier daemon and confirm it emits a distinct finished signal to the parent instead of an ambiguous waiting. An ordinary turn with no sentinel records no completion.
capabilities.assertion: the Stop-hook persists done status=ok, and the daemon emits a finished event carrying that outcome (issue #1186)
capabilities.snapshot:
hook status file (what the Stop-hook persisted):
{
  "status": "waiting",
  "event": "Stop",
  "done_status": "ok",
  "done_summary": "capability wave2 shipped"
}

finished event emitted by notify-daemon (the [DONE] signal to the parent):
child_title: cap-child
child_session_id: 0a81e066-1779906947
kind: finished
done_status: ok
done_summary: capability wave2 shipped

capabilities.title: An idle worker does not re-notify
capabilities.how_we_test: We make a worker transition once, then run the notifier daemon twice over the same idle worker. We confirm exactly one notification is produced across both passes, proving the de-duplication ledger persists between polls.
capabilities.assertion: two daemon passes over the same idle child emit exactly one event (issue #1187 dedup)
capabilities.snapshot:
two `notify-daemon --once` passes over the same idle child
emitted exactly one transition event (the second was deduped):

child_title: dd-child
child_session_id: 5b085245-1779906947
from_status: running
to_status: idle
